_publications._publicationTitle: Identifying Symptoms of Distress in Youth Living with Neurofibromatosis Type 1 (NF1).

_contextSnippet: Caregiver/patient and provider/patient concordance were both quite poor. Only one age group (13–17 year olds) exhibited a significant, but modest, correlation in reported distress levels between the caregivers and their child (r = 0.41, p < 0.05).
details: Poor concordance between caregivers and patients, with only 13-17 year olds showing significant but modest correlation (r = 0.41, p < 0.05). No significant provider/patient correlation for any age groups
